LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF IRREPARABLE HARM. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THERE IS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AT TRIAL. A MANDATORY INJUNCTION PENDING TRIAL IS NOT PERMITTED EXCEPT IN EXTREME CASES WHERE THE RIGHT IS CLEARLY ESTABLISHED. SEE TEACHERS INS. & ANNUITY ASSN. VS. FURLOTTI (1999) 70 CAL.APP. 4TH 1487, 1493. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff Cocoa Development Retail, Llc'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
APPEARANCES: David S. Reidy, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP 659-5933 for pltf & moving party; Michael Waughtel, Esq. of Hornstein Law Offices 454-1490 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff fails to show a likelihood of irreparable harm. Insufficient showing that there is a reasonable probability that plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial. A mandatory injunction pending trial is not permitted except in extreme caSes where the right is clearly established. See Teachers Ins. & Annuity Assn. vs. Furlotti (1999) 70 Cal.App. 4th 1487, 1493. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
